sections.label: Software
sections.title: Software
paragraphs: Neuroartan software is the execution layer where ICOS product logic becomes usable application infrastructure.
The software layer connects interface, runtime behavior, model routing, profile continuity, and governed user experience into one operational system.
Its purpose is to turn product doctrine into stable, testable, and expandable software surfaces.

sections.label: Runtime Architecture
sections.title: Runtime Architecture
paragraphs: Runtime architecture defines how the application loads models, manages interaction state, and streams responses through the product interface.
The system is designed to support a stable path from app shell to runtime manager, model registry, inference engine, and response surface.
This keeps implementation aligned with the long-term ICOS product architecture.

sections.label: Application Shell
sections.title: Application Shell
paragraphs: The application shell provides the visible product environment for capture, review, profile interaction, and settings control.
It must remain coherent across web, app, and future software layers so users experience ICOS as one system rather than fragmented tools.
The shell is where interface discipline, accessibility, motion, and continuity meet.

sections.label: Model Routing
sections.title: Model Routing
paragraphs: Model routing allows ICOS to select the appropriate intelligence layer while preserving product-level control.
The routing system can evolve between external APIs, local models, and embedded runtime paths without changing the user's core continuity experience.
This makes software architecture flexible without making product behavior unstable.

sections.label: Profile Continuity
sections.title: Profile Continuity
paragraphs: Profile continuity connects software state with long-term user memory, settings, permissions, and reflection history.
It ensures that product intelligence remains anchored in the user profile rather than isolated session fragments.
This is the layer that makes ICOS durable across time, devices, and future product surfaces.
